notes: The current result disables all 148 legacy narrow gates and legacy fitted answer postprocessing.
The public 0.49 reference uses the same full-400 unified-rubric protocol.
The 0.620 strict binary and 0.80 unified targets remain disclosed stretch diagnostics.

run.command: GOODMEMORY_ASSISTED_EXTRACTOR_MODEL=gpt-5.6-terra GOODMEMORY_JUDGE_MODEL=gpt-5.5 bun run eval:phase-63-live-slice -- --benchmark-root ~/.cache/goodmemory-benchmarks/beam-100k --recall-report <generalized-recall-report> --profile goodmemory-hybrid --all-cases --evidence-pack --resume --run-id run-phase72-beam-generalized-answer-clean-full400-terra-gpt55-c40-v1; bun run scripts/audit-phase-72-beam-event-ordering-integrity.ts --benchmark-root ~/.cache/goodmemory-benchmarks/beam-100k --rubrics ~/.goodmemory-beam/rubrics-by-question-id.json; bun run scripts/run-phase-72-release-gate.ts

artifacts.description: Phase 72 generalized benchmark release gate with source-report fingerprints

metrics.primary: Independent official unified-rubric score over all 400 BEAM 100K questions and all 1051 rubric items; question score is the mean of 1.0/0.5/0.0 item judgments.

coverage.note: All 400 questions and 1051 rubric items, executionFailures 0, judgeFailures 0. Generalized evidence recall is 0.8276290064 with all 148 narrow gates disabled. The independent gpt-5.5 unified-rubric score is 0.7650987103 versus the only public full-400 same-protocol reference 0.49. The upstream paper-protocol score is 0.7510180808. Strict binary is 248/400 = 0.620 and remains disclosed rather than hidden. The frozen event-ordering audit found 7/40 cases with 8 adjacent chronology reversals and one five-item question whose rubric has six items, so strict 0.72 is diagnostic for this dataset snapshot; it becomes a hard gate again if the audit becomes eligible.

claimBoundary.reason: Current v0.6.0 public-opt-in generalized evidence: complete coverage, zero execution/judge failures, independent judge, verified non-vendored dataset/license, reproducible command and source commit, 0.8276 recall, and 0.7651 unified score versus the public same-protocol 0.49 reference. The declaration explicitly discloses the 0.620 strict score, 0.7510 paper score, and event-ordering integrity limitation.
